title: Security Researchers Warn a Widely Used Open Source Tool Poses a 'Persistent' Risk to the US
body:
Hello, I have been notified of this story: https://www.wired.com/story/easyjson-open-source-vk-ties/

And noticed podman is using easyjson so am wondering if there are plans to replace this module and what they are?

comments.body:
Podman doesn't use it directly. It seems to get pulled in by github.com/go-openapi/swag (https://github.com/go-openapi/swag/issues/68) which it turn is pulled in by github.com/sigstore/rekor AFAICT.

As so it happen I do have some plan to hopefully remove github.com/sigstore/rekor (i.e. https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1845) for other reasons which seems to be enough to remove it out of the direct dependency chain so I hope I get around to it no matter what.

In any case it is likely best to get the repos that directly depend on it to migrate but that assumes that there is anything actually wrong with the code which doesn't sounds like there is

Okay thank you @Luap99  that makes complete sense and we're in agreement. So I think it's just a matter of getting ahead of it and reducing overall risk potential.

Do you have any timeline on your PR? I noticed its been open for a couple of years.

The PR is just an example what we can do (it was never prioritized), plan is to finish it that by end of the quarter as it was assigned to me but I get sidetracked with other work currently so I won't commit to that.
The OpenAPI folks are also investigating removing it - https://github.com/go-openapi/swag/issues/68
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
